Problem 1
Based on the definition of each feature of a circle below, label the circle with the appropriate vocabulary words.
Arc:
Part of any curve
Central angle:
An angle formed by two radii with the vertex as the center of the circle
Chord:
A line segment containing two points on a curve
Diameter:
A line segment connecting two points on a curve that passes through the center of the circle
Radius:
A line segment that connects the center of a circle and a point on the circle
Tangent:
A line that intersect a circle at one and only one point

khan_exercises.title: Complementary and supplementary angles (no visual)
khan_exercises.description: Use complementary and supplementary angles to solve for a missing angle measure.

items.question_content:
$\angle a$ and $\angle b$ are complementary angles.  $\angle a$ measures $22^\circ$.  

**What is the measure of $\angle b$?**  


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\Large{^\circ}$
hints:
Here is a picture of what $\angle a$ and $\angle b$ could look like: 

[[☃ image 1]]
Complementary angles combine to create a right angle.  So, they have a sum of $90^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\angle a + \angle b &= 90^\circ
\\\\
22^\circ+\angle b &= 90^\circ
 \\\\
\angle b &= 68^\circ \end{align}$

items.question_content:

options.alt: A right angle is divided into 2 equal parts, labeled as 35 degrees and 5 X degrees.

khan_exercises.title: Complementary and supplementary angles (no visual)
khan_exercises.description: Use complementary and supplementary angles to solve for a missing angle measure.

items.question_content:
$\angle a$ and $\angle b$ are complementary angles.  $\angle a$ measures $22^\circ$.  

**What is the measure of $\angle b$?**  


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\Large{^\circ}$
hints:
Here is a picture of what $\angle a$ and $\angle b$ could look like: 

[[☃ image 1]]
Complementary angles combine to create a right angle.  So, they have a sum of $90^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\angle a + \angle b &= 90^\circ
\\\\
22^\circ+\angle b &= 90^\circ
 \\\\
\angle b &= 68^\circ \end{align}$
